title: cron_create_test invokes process-global cwd mutation poisoning sibling tests
description:
## Severity
Trivial

## Dimension
Testability / Concurrency

## Location
src/tools/cron.rs:395-503 (cron tests that std::env::set_current_dir into tempdirs)

## Violation
Five cron tests mutate process-global cwd via std::env::set_current_dir(tmp.path()) (lines 402, 434, 473, 514, 550) — even though the function under test (ScheduleStore::load/save) is only "stuck on cwd" because its SCHEDULES_FILE is a relative path. Tests carry the same workaround the production code requires: a serializing cwd_lock that pretends to coordinate but is in fact independent of worktree.rs's separate cwd_lock.

This makes the entire test module a global concurrency hazard. Any new test in a separate module that mutates cwd without taking BOTH locks corrupts cron tests. The locks are an aspirin for the underlying problem.

The root cause is cron's relative-path file (see separate issue on SCHEDULES_FILE being relative). Fixing the production code eliminates the need for these tests to touch cwd at all.

## Mandated refactor
After making SCHEDULES_FILE absolute (via PROJECT_ROOT or a constructor argument), refactor all five tests to pass the desired storage path in directly (via a ScheduleStore::with_path(tmp.path().join("schedules.json")) constructor). The cwd dance disappears; tests can run in parallel without serialization.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
